summary: axois-utils postinstall exfiltrates host/user metadata via HTTP
details: The package name is a single-character typosquat of the widely-used 'axios' HTTP client. On install, postinstall.js requires child_process and uses http.request to POST host metadata to an external endpoint. The collected fields include os.hostname(), username, and cwd, gathered at install time and sent off-host. This is the canonical install-time reconnaissance/exfiltration shape, with no legitimate purpose for a utility named to imitate axios. The combination of name-confusion attack against a top-tier package and an active install-time data-collection-and-POST primitive in a lifecycle script is unambiguous installer-side harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
